summary: Malignant pustule typically denotes a pustular lesion resulting from an underlying skin cancer, most commonly through invasion and necrotic degeneration causing suppurative discharge. Infected squamous cell carcinoma fits this definition because it combines a primary malignant process (squamous cell carcinoma) with a pustular presentation due to tissue destruction and infection, representing the closest match among listed options.
